title: Quadlet container stucks in stopping state
body:
### Issue Description

I am trying to achieve the same behavior as with `podman stop -t 0 <nameOrId>` for a Quadlet container (I want to stop it immediately, regardless of its configuration). To do this, I use `systemctl --user kill --signal=SIGKILL <service>`. However, I am experiencing an issue while running this command when the container is already in stopping state, triggered by `systemctl --user stop <service>` . The container stays in the stopping state forever.

Since @mheon mentioned that [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19629#issuecomment-1711747006) is expected behaviour I have also have created a [question](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/26639) in the Discussion section.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a quadlet container unit file `~/.config/containers/systemd/test.container` with following content: 
```
[Container]
Image=alpine
ContainerName=test
Exec=sleep 1000

[Service]
TimeoutStartSec=600
``` 
2. Do `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
3. Do `systemctl --user start test`
4. Do `systemctl --user stop test`
5. In the other terminal tab do `systemctl --user kill --signal=SIGKILL test`
6. Do `podman ps -a`


### Describe the results you received

After doing `podman ps -a` container stays in the `stopping` state:

```
322847aaa139  docker.io/library/alpine:latest             sleep 1000              33 seconds ago       Stopping                           test
```

and the systemd service status:

```
× test.service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/vova/.config/containers/systemd/test.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /etc/systemd/user/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: failed (Result: signal) since Tue 2025-07-15 11:22:21 CEST; 53min ago
   Duration: 16.652s
 Invocation: fdec367daf91437fabe74e5f0504efe5
    Process: 2510870 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name test --cidfile=/run/user/1000/test.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d alpine sleep 1000 (code=killed, signal=KILL)
    Process: 2511073 ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1000/test.cid (code=killed, signal=KILL)
    Process: 2511160 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1000/test.cid (code=killed, signal=KILL)
   Main PID: 2510870 (code=killed, signal=KILL)
   Mem peak: 45.8M
        CPU: 306ms
```

### Describe the results you expected

There is no container in the list after doing `podman ps -a`

### podman info output

comments.body:
My recommendation would be to set KillSignal/StopSignal https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/26639#discussioncomment-13763143

If systemctl kill also kills the ExecStopPost then yeah we cannot perform cleanup and leak resources.
Reading the systemctl man page it seems there is the option to use `--kill-whom=main` on `systemctl kill` which should only kill the main process and still allow our cleanup process to run correctly then I guess. So try that maybe.

I have already tried `systemctl --user kill --kill-whom=main --signal=SIGKILL test` and the container still stays, but at least it is not in the `stopping` state:

```
75f8cbbdbf10  docker.io/library/alpine:latest      sleep 1000       About a minute ago    Exited (-1) 8 seconds ago     test            
```

The reason why we want to avoid using `StopSignal` with `SIGKILL` is because we want to have two options: stop the container normally (if the proper handling of the `SIGTERM` was configured) or do the force shutdown without waiting till the `--stop-timeout` exceeds. It seems that in case of quadlet units it is impossible to overwrite that behaviour after the systemd container service is up.

> I have already tried systemctl --user kill --kill-whom=main --signal=SIGKILL test and the container still stays, but at least it is not in the stopping state:

Mhh, weird. If we can convert it to exited I am not sure why it was not removed. Does the ExecStopPost= command ran in this case?


> The reason why we want to avoid using StopSignal with SIGKILL is because we want to have two options: stop the container normally (if the proper handling of the SIGTERM was configured) or do the force shutdown without waiting till the --stop-timeout exceeds. It seems that in case of quadlet units it is impossible to overwrite that behaviour after the systemd container service is up.

I mean you can always directly send kill -9 to the main container pid or use `podman kill` on the container which should allow you to force the shutdown right away.

> Mhh, weird. If we can convert it to exited I am not sure why it was not removed. Does the ExecStopPost= command ran in this case?

Yes it did but it exited with exit code `125`

```
× test.service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/vova/.config/containers/systemd/test.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /etc/systemd/user/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: failed (Result: signal) since Tue 2025-07-15 14:48:27 CEST; 3s ago
   Duration: 5.789s
 Invocation: 9cfbad6063bb4dc38b929a4d6758f564
    Process: 2699991 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name test --cidfile=/run/user/1000/test.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d alpine sleep 1000 (code=killed, signal=KILL)
    Process: 2700025 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1000/test.cid (code=exited, status=125)
   Main PID: 2699991 (code=killed, signal=KILL)
   Mem peak: 45.5M
        CPU: 243ms

Jul 15 14:48:21 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: Started test.service.
Jul 15 14:48:21 dhcp-10-29-246-140 test[2699960]: f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: test.service: Sent signal SIGKILL to main process 2699991 (conmon) on client request.
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: test.service: Main process exited, code=killed, status=9/KILL
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: test.service: Killing process 2699993 (sleep) with signal SIGKILL.
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: test.service: Killing process 2699989 (pasta.avx2) with signal SIGKILL.
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 podman[2700025]: 2025-07-15 14:48:27.408094156 +0200 CEST m=+0.071154365 container stop f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 (image=docker.io/librar>
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 podman[2700025]: 2025-07-15 14:48:27.408504613 +0200 CEST m=+0.071564822 container died f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 (image=docker.io/librar>
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 test[2700025]: Error: cannot remove container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 as it could not be stopped: container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb>
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 systemd[2487]: test.service: Failed with result 'signal'.
```

> I mean you can always directly send kill -9 to the main container pid or use podman kill on the container which should allow you to force the shutdown right away.

In our case, we cannot use `kill -9` because the process to be killed is not in the same process namespace in which we do the container orchestration.

> Error: cannot remove container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 as it could not be stopped: container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb>

Can you provide the full error text. This seem like a legit podman bug if we are unable to remove the container after we conmon got killed that we should be able to fix. That should be enough to make `--kill-whom=main` work properly then.

Sure, here it is 
```
Jul 15 14:48:27 dhcp-10-29-246-140 test[2700025]: Error: cannot remove container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 as it could not be stopped: container f63591faf9252cbb970e2404ceb77b72bd895f2ce17ca6d1abbc11eb0143a060 conmon exited prematurely, exit code could not be retrieved: internal libpod error
```
Feel free to give https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26643 a try, I it should fix the error and correctly remove the container when conmon is killed

> Feel free to give https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26643 a try, I it should fix the error and correctly remove the container when conmon is killed

That works, thank you
